author: @matthewchambers152
text:
Hey Robert. Since starting to watch your videos I've lined up a 3D printer and a friend who's interested to see if I can get his farmhouse off the power grid. So, I'm chomping at the bit to see where you go next with the vertical wind generators. I'm keen to see if I can build an income for myself with the amazing stuff I've learnt from your channel.
I wanted to ask, the farm has an average wind speed of 9mps. Do I need to consider using a thicker wire? Seems like most of the testing your doing is around 4mps.
Cheers and thank you. It feels like it's been a long time since I've been so thoroughly engaged in learning something new.

author: @ThomasAndersonbsf
text:
NICE! I bought two vacuum pump (has inlet for sucking and outlet for dispersing into a chamber) made for stuff like removing oil and transmission fluid because I am working on an automatic pickup truck transmission AND I saw the value of having a completely clean one to build a pumped water filtration system that I plan on building, I see as the filters get full a bit of pressure or suction could speed up water being passed through as well as maybe other options since weight of excess water pressure in the dirty side tank speeds up the initial water passing through. 

I really like the idea of the suction here you show of the air pulling water into the tank, awesome and this seems like an even better solution both for moving water around and maybe build that water filtration system for better living you might need (I keep seeing people buying bottled water, on pallets and it seems to me the smarter option would be to stop after you have a pallet of them, keep the bottles clean and just get a water filter for pennies on the dollar for water you filter yourself, then refill those bottles if you just have to have the convienence of some small water bottles to carry around or something, but hey try getting them to spend 30 bucks on a filter one time with a 15 dollar water filter cartridge every 5 months when they can spend 15 bucks on water bottles every two weeks right? LOLOL) 

anyway, I really want to see more on nano particle production, Have you tried working with other metals or even how to decompose oxidized ones like aluminum to make solid aluminum coating? 

asking because I think the metal nano particle production and deployment in those graphene foam from paper under a laser would be possibly a game changer to go from super capacitor to super battery types. 

****with renewable activated carbon filters based on the "ZERO" water filtration system as its the best one I have found so far and you can actually clean the activated particles of the material it held onto, by flushing warm salt water through (the smell is horrible as it has pulled concentrated filth it caught from the tap water so it smells in some places I have lived like a mixture of road tar cooking and dirty cigarette ash tray nonsense, other places the smell is different, but it really gives an idea of how horrible the water you drink actually is seeing it concentrated)

author: @johnbarry8185
text: Rob, you didn't need the bellows.  Just use a section of pipe, capped off, that slides into a larger pipe that goes into the tank.  You can use either the pipe alone, or a pipe with a rubber piece on the bottom of it, or a myriad of other devices.  Keep up the good work.

author: @jamest.5001
text:
It needs a spring inside the bellows! 
Also a piston and lever will work, made from a stick of pvc pipe, probably 50mm or larger, or multiple smaller pieces,  using a dowel rod, 30mm or so, as long as it fits in the pipe.  Then a pair of washers that snuggly  fit the pipe inside, yet move freely,   then cut a piece of rubber that will fit the inside of the pipe slightly larger than the washers,  make atleast  two of these, cut about 20-30mm of dowel, drill a hole in the center, put the first washerover a 2" plus wood  screw, then rubber, washer, short bit of dowel , washer, rubber, then  washer, then place the dowel inside the pipe with the dowel 200mm + longer than the pipe
Then screw the washers, rubber and dowel bit into the rod, after placing them inside the pipe so all us sonewhat  centered, there it is, a piston pump. Use the valves and all, basically the same way, with 300mm-600mm of pipe or more, 30-50mm or larger, maybe use a bit of vegetable oil to lube

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: no it doesn't the bellows is it's own spring - and yes a piston works as well - but badly unless the piston fit is good - and that is harder to do than it sounds

author: @mrab4222
text: If it's difficult to lift the bellows, try turning it upside-down so that you can use your weight to expand it. If fact, if you connected the bellows with a flexible pipe, you would be able to flip it either way up and use your weight either way. One way up, use your weight to expand it, other way up, use your weight to compress it. Alternatively, put it on one side of a rocker. Weight on one side to expand, weight on the other side to compress.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: or just put a lever on it

author: @bikerfirefarter7280
text:
You're a strong lad managing to shift that concertina at all. I tried to pump water out of a flooded pit using a foot-bellows (the type used to inflate airbeds). With only a 2.4m head my whole body weight (13st) could't compress the bellows at all. The area of the pump face is just too high, I had to use an old stirrup pump; that was easy to pump the 2.4m head, but it took ages due to the limited volume of each stroke. I couldn't use a submersible pump, as we were in the middle of nowhere at the time.
As an aside; If you lay an airbed between two sheets of thick plywood, you can lift actuall TONS using lung power; but better yet you can lift ENORMOUS weights using mains water-pressure (that comes in handy if you are underpinning a house etc.)

author: @JFSmith-nb8hf
text: On a river, a water wheel could be rigged to run the pump. A self powered water system.

replies.author: @Smo1k
replies.text: There's an ancient irrigation system relying on a waterwheel to do the lifting. No pump involved, the blades of the wheel simply do not let go of all the water until they're near vertical (and above the river, natch), at which point the water runs off into a tray.

replies.author: @SeaforgedArtifacts
replies.text: Unfortunately, there are very few such rivers in Lahaina.

Turbines?

replies.author: @ulisesgarcia8054
replies.text: Maybe a foolish idea, but wouldn't it be crazy to pump water into a Electric Generator Turbine to power an Atmospheric Water Generator to increase drinkable water? If the flow of rivers could be increased enough, maybe you could power a Desalination Plant. Maybe you won't have enough electricity to power a home, but you'd have water to drink and minerals to be used in chemistry like Lithium or Sodium.

replies.author: @SeaforgedArtifacts
replies.text: @ulisesgarcia8054 interesting concept, disregarding the energy, you are investing energy into water and investing water into more water. If you could keep the water contained that would create a feedback loop that ultimately would start to produce a surplus and change the whole local ecosystem, no?
